file_name: SmartRxSystemsInc_20180914_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_11351705_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_Distributor Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: Products shall mean the Company's Smart Pharm Assist Kiosk™ and associated documentation, inventions, software, application and processes.
perturbation.changed_text: Products shall mean solely the Company's Smart Pharm Assist Kiosk™ hardware. All associated documentation, inventions, software, applications and processes are explicitly excluded from this definition.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the definition of 'Products'. Initially, it included the Kiosk and its associated elements. Now, it solely refers to the Kiosk hardware, excluding software and documentation. This could lead to disputes over responsibilities regarding software updates, documentation accuracy, and intellectual property rights connected to elements beyond the hardware itself. This contradicts section 5, which discusses intellectual property rights related to the Products, since the 'Products' now have a narrower definition, so the intellectual property ownership scope becomes vague.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except for the limited license granted in subsection 5.1 above, Distributor acknowledges and agrees that Company or its licensors own and will continue to own all right, title, and interest in and to the Products, including all copyright rights (including rights in audiovisual works), moral rights, patent rights (including patent applications and disclosures), rights of priority and trade secret rights recognized in any country or jurisdiction of the world, regardless of whether the Products are separate or combined with any other products, programs, or data. Company may, at its option, provide Distributor with copies of the Products for demonstration purposes, ill such cases, the demonstration products shall remain the property of Company and may not be duplicated, resold, lent, leased, given away or otherwise distributed. Distributor agrees to return aforesaid software at its expense upon written request from Company or at the termination of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Products' to solely the hardware, explicitly excluding associated documentation, software, and processes. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which grants the Company ownership of intellectual property rights related to 'Products,' including copyrights and patent rights, which would typically encompass software and documentation. The new, narrower definition creates a clear contradiction because the company claims ownership over IP rights to aspects now excluded from the definition of 'Products'. This is a strong contradiction due to the potential legal implications regarding intellectual property ownership and scope, and it follows the provided definition of an ambiguity in text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Territory shall mean Exclusivity for the state of Texas, USA, and Exclusive Distribution opportunities in all other states (with either the Company or Distributor), except the state of Florida.
perturbation.changed_text: Territory shall mean Exclusivity for the state of Texas, USA. Distribution opportunities in all other states will be determined on a case-by-case basis and are subject to Company's final approval, except the state of Florida, where the company reserves the right to operate autonomously.
perturbation.explanation: The original text provides for 'Exclusive Distribution opportunities' in other states (excluding Florida) with either the Company or Distributor. The modified text eliminates the 'Exclusive Distribution opportunities' and makes it subject to 'Company's final approval on a case-by-case basis'. This creates an in-text contradiction. This change contradicts section 2.1 where distributor is appointed as exclusive for the state of Texas and other states, that now there is no distributor can be appointed for other states besides Texas.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exclusive Appointment. Company hereby appoints Distributor as Company's exclusive Distributor for the State of Texas, and Distributor accepts the appointment and agrees to represent the Products within the Territory. Company agrees that no other Distributor will be appointed in any other state as a Distributor unless it is either the Company or Distributor, save and except for the state of Florida.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated 'Exclusive Distribution opportunities' in states other than Texas and Florida, with either the Company or Distributor. The changed text alters this by making distribution opportunities subject to the Company's final approval on a case-by-case basis, eliminating the 'Exclusive Distribution opportunities' for states other than Texas. This contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which appoints the Distributor as the exclusive distributor for Texas and states that no other distributor will be appointed in other states besides the company or distributor, save for Florida. The change removes the distributors entitlement to be an exclusive distributor in other states, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor agrees to use commercially reasonable efforts to market Company's Products in the Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor is expected to use maximum effort to market Company's Products in the Territory, meeting KPIs established by the company, which may be revised by the company on a quarterly basis.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by altering the effort level required for marketing. Initially, 'commercially reasonable efforts' were required. Now, 'maximum effort' is expected, tied to KPIs that the company can change quarterly. This introduces ambiguity because 'maximum effort' is subjective and subject to potentially unreasonable KPIs set by the company. This contradiction may hinder the Distributor's right of operating the business under Section 7, where the distributor is free to chose the manner it seems fit to conduct business.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distributor will conduct all its business in its own name and in such manner as it may see fit. However, conduct which Company, in its discretion, deems detrimental to Company's image or reputation, shall be grounds for termination of this Agreement, upon reasonable notice and the failure to cure such behavior by Distributor.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'commercially reasonable efforts' to 'maximum effort' tied to company-controlled KPIs directly contradicts the distributor's right to conduct business 'in such manner as it may see fit,' as stated in the contradicted text. The company's ability to revise KPIs quarterly introduces uncertainty and undermines the distributor's autonomy, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction. The 'maximum effort' expected to meet KPIs limits the distributors freedom from Section 7.
